Agent2 Round 1: Anaerobic glycolysis refers specifically to the process where glucose undergoes fermentation when oxygen is not available. The net yield of ATP produced during anaerobic glycolysis from one molecule of glucose is approximately two ATPs due to substrate-level phosphorylation steps but no oxidative phosphorylation occurs since there's no electron transport chain functioning in low-oxygen conditions. - Each Pyruvate → Lactic Acid (additional ~1 ATP via lactic dehydrogenase reaction).
